knownIssues.description: Firefox MIME type blocking on /catalog page (JS module loading error). Blank page rendered. Known issue from Suite 09.
knownIssues.impact: Blocks catalog page testing in Firefox only. Not white-labeling specific.
knownIssues.consoleError: Loading module from 'https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/assets/index-DT1lESh8.js' was blocked because of a disallowed MIME type ('text/html')

bugs.title: Logo alt text is generic 'B2B-store' instead of organization name
bugs.description: The header logo alt text is 'B2B-store' regardless of the active organization. When 'Coffee shop' org is active with a custom logo, the alt text should reflect the organization name (e.g., 'Coffee shop logo'). This impacts screen reader users who cannot distinguish between organizations via logo alt text.
bugs.expected: Logo alt text should be the organization name or a descriptive text like '<OrgName> Logo'
bugs.actual: Logo alt text is always 'B2B-store' (store name) even when org-specific logo is displayed

bugs.title: Footer navigation lacks aria-label attribute
bugs.description: The <nav> element in the footer section has no aria-label attribute. This makes it indistinguishable from the main navigation for screen reader users. Should have aria-label='Footer navigation' or similar.
bugs.expected: Footer <nav> should have aria-label='Footer navigation' or equivalent
bugs.actual: Footer <nav> has no aria-label and no role attribute

bugs.title: 17 images on homepage lack alt text and are not aria-hidden
bugs.description: 17 elements on the homepage have no alt attribute and are not marked with aria-hidden='true'. These include decorative icons and UI elements that should either have descriptive alt text or be explicitly hidden from assistive technology.
bugs.expected: All images should have alt text (descriptive for informative images) or aria-hidden='true' (for decorative images)
bugs.actual: 17 images lack both alt and aria-hidden attributes

testResults.title: Enable thumbnail job for favicons
testResults.reason: Backend admin job test - requires Admin SPA access and job execution. Out of scope for storefront-only frontend testing in this run.

testResults.title: Store and organization have no whitelabeling settings - default branding displayed
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA verification of store/org settings. Cannot verify 'no settings configured' state from storefront alone.

testResults.title: Store has whitelabeling settings but organization does not - store defaults shown
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to verify org has no custom settings. Partially verified: both 'Bence and Family' and 'Coffee shop' orgs show branding, suggesting store-level settings are active.

testResults.title: Store and organization both have whitelabeling settings - organization branding overrides store
testResults.notes: Verified: Switching from 'Bence and Family' to 'Coffee shop' org changed the header logo from file API (e24006ee...) to CMS content (logo_cfe0eaef...). Organization branding overrides store-level logo correctly. Theme CSS variables remained Coffee theme (#996c5a primary-500).

testResults.title: Set non-existent theme preset - default theme applied as fallback
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to set invalid theme preset name. Cannot configure from storefront.

testResults.title: Set custom logo and favicon for organization - displayed after sign-in
testResults.notes: Verified: Coffee shop org displays custom logo (from vcst-qa.govirto.com/cms-content/assets/customization/). Logo is 900x593px natural, rendered at 68x45px. Favicon present with 3 sizes (SVG, 16x16 PNG, 32x32 PNG). Logo is clear and not pixelated.

testResults.title: Set White Labeling Enabled = OFF for organization - default logo and favicon displayed
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to toggle White Labeling off for org.

testResults.title: Set White Labeling Enabled = ON for organization - custom logo and favicon displayed
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to toggle White Labeling on.

testResults.title: Store-level White Labeling = OFF - organization branding disabled regardless of org settings
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA store-level WL toggle.

testResults.title: Set theme preset for organization - Coffee theme applied after sign-in
testResults.notes: Verified: Coffee theme active. CSS variables confirm Coffee palette: --color-primary-500: #996c5a (brown), --color-primary-600: #7a5648, --color-secondary-500: #637780, --color-accent-500: #447d9c. Header background: rgb(61, 43, 36) (dark brown). 175 CSS custom properties loaded.

testResults.title: Add parent footer links for organization - displayed in storefront footer
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer displays 7 parent sections: Account details (3 links), About us (6 links), Popular categories (5 links), Online resources (4 links), External links (2 links), Customer support (3 links), Brands (1 link). Total 24 footer links. Hierarchical structure present.

testResults.title: Add children footer links under parent - hierarchical structure displayed
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer has hierarchical structure. Each parent section contains child links. For example, 'About us' parent has children: About Virto, Our partners, Manufacturer partners, News, Careers, News. Links navigate to correct URLs.

testResults.title: Verify footer links returned in whiteLabelingSettings GraphQL query
testResults.reason: Requires direct GraphQL playground access. Network tab confirms GraphQL calls return 200, but cannot inspect individual query payloads in this session.

testResults.title: Set footer link list for organization - footer links displayed after sign-in
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer links are displayed for Coffee shop org. 7 sections with 24 total links. Footer structure matches store-level configuration (same sections as Bence and Family - indicating these may be store-level footer links, not org-specific overrides).

testResults.title: Verify administrator role has access to White Labeling logo management
testResults.reason: Admin SPA role verification - out of scope for storefront testing.

testResults.title: Verify organization logo displayed in header after sign-in
testResults.notes: Verified: Bence and Family org shows logo from /api/files/e24006ee... (900x593 native, 68x45 rendered). Coffee shop org shows different logo from /cms-content/assets/customization/logo_cfe0eaef... Logo is clickable, links to homepage (/). Both logos are clear and properly sized.

testResults.title: Verify organization favicon displayed in browser tab after sign-in
testResults.notes: Verified: 3 favicon link elements present in DOM: SVG icon (primary), 16x16 PNG, 32x32 PNG. All loaded as data URIs (inline base64). Favicon displays the Virto Commerce colored logo (yellow/red geometric shapes). Note: favicon appears to be the same Virto default for both orgs tested - not org-specific.

testResults.title: Verify organization secondary logo displayed (if configured)
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer displays a secondary logo (white variant: logo-white-C86xQ0EF.svg) with alt='B2B-store'. This is a static asset, not org-specific. The white logo variant is used in the dark footer area.

testResults.title: Verify theme preset colors applied to storefront after sign-in - Default theme
testResults.reason: Both tested orgs use Coffee theme. Would need an org with Default theme to compare.

testResults.title: Verify theme preset colors applied to storefront after sign-in - Coffee theme
testResults.notes: Verified: Coffee theme applied. Primary colors: #996c5a (brown-500), #7a5648 (brown-600). Secondary: #637780. Accent: #447d9c. Header bg: rgb(61,43,36) dark brown. Brown/cream palette visible across header, buttons, links. Consistent across pages.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links displayed in header after sign-in (NEW - VCST-4637)
testResults.reason: The current storefront displays catalog categories in the main navigation bar (Alcoholic Drinks, Accessories, Jewelry and gems, etc.), NOT custom main menu links from a link list. This suggests either: (a) MainMenuLinkListName is not configured for the tested orgs, or (b) the main menu links feature renders differently than expected. The navigation shows 15 catalog category items. Cannot verify VCST-4637 main menu link list feature without Admin SPA configuration.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links hierarchical dropdown behavior (parent + children)
testResults.reason: Blocked by FWL-021. Main menu shows catalog categories, not custom link list. Cannot test hierarchical dropdown of custom main menu links.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links on mobile - hamburger menu with hierarchy
testResults.notes: Verified at 375px: Hamburger menu (3-line icon) visible and functional. Mobile sidebar opens showing: org name 'Coffee shop', theme toggle, language selector. Navigation links: Dashboard, Catalog, Compare, Bulk order, Cart, Contact us. Account section with Purchasing/Marketing/Corporate/User sub-menus. Mobile menu is hierarchical with expand/collapse. Note: These are account navigation links, not custom main menu links from VCST-4637.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links navigation works correctly
testResults.reason: Blocked by FWL-021. Cannot test custom main menu link navigation without configured links.

testResults.title: Verify fallback when no main menu link list configured for organization
testResults.notes: Verified: With no MainMenuLinkListName configured, the storefront gracefully falls back to showing catalog categories in the navigation bar. No crash, no error, no broken layout. All other navigation elements (search, cart, account) work correctly. Footer links display normally.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links returned in whiteLabelingSettings GraphQL query (VCST-4637)
testResults.reason: Requires GraphQL playground access to execute query directly.

testResults.title: Verify pageContext query includes whiteLabelingSettings with mainMenuLinks
testResults.reason: Requires GraphQL playground access to execute pageContext query.

testResults.title: Switch organization - verify branding updates dynamically (logo and favicon)
testResults.notes: Verified: Switched from 'Bence and Family' to 'Coffee shop'. Logo changed from /api/files/e24006ee... to /cms-content/assets/customization/logo_cfe0eaef... Org name updated in header and next to logo. Ship-to address changed (org-specific addresses). Cart count reset (org-specific cart). Transition was smooth with no broken images or layout issues.

testResults.title: Switch organization - verify main menu links update dynamically
testResults.reason: Blocked by FWL-021. Main menu shows catalog categories for both orgs. Cannot verify custom main menu link switching.

testResults.title: Switch organization - verify footer links update dynamically
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer links remained the same structure (7 sections, 24 links) after switching from 'Bence and Family' to 'Coffee shop'. This suggests both orgs use the same store-level footer link list, or both orgs have the same footer configuration. Footer rendered correctly in both contexts.

testResults.title: Switch organization - verify theme preset changes dynamically
testResults.notes: Verified: Theme CSS variables remained the same Coffee theme (#996c5a) for both orgs. Both 'Bence and Family' and 'Coffee shop' use Coffee theme preset. Theme is applied consistently. To fully verify dynamic theme switching, would need orgs with different theme presets.

testResults.title: Verify branding transition is smooth during organization switch (no FOUC)
testResults.notes: Verified: No flash of wrong branding during org switch. Logo transition was smooth. Page reloaded with new org context. No broken images or layout during transition. No console errors during switch. Theme colors remained consistent (both orgs have same theme).

testResults.title: Verify logo renders correctly on mobile viewport (375px - iPhone)
testResults.notes: Verified at 375px: Coffee shop logo (coffee bean icon) visible in mobile header. Logo is appropriately sized for mobile (small icon, not oversized). Logo is clear and not pixelated. Logo is positioned correctly alongside hamburger menu. Does not overlap other mobile header elements.

testResults.title: Verify main menu links accessible via hamburger menu on mobile
testResults.notes: Verified: Hamburger menu opens mobile drawer. Navigation links (Dashboard, Catalog, Compare, Bulk order, Cart, Contact us) accessible. Account sub-menus (Purchasing, Marketing, Corporate, User) with expand/collapse. Org name displayed. Note: These are standard navigation links, not custom main menu links from VCST-4637.

testResults.title: Verify footer links display correctly on mobile viewport
testResults.notes: Verified at 375px: Footer sections converted to accordion/collapsible pattern (buttons with expand/collapse). All 7 sections present: Account details, About us, Popular categories, Online resources, External links, Customer support, Brands. Mobile-optimized layout.

testResults.title: Verify theme colors applied correctly on mobile viewport
testResults.notes: Verified at 375px: Coffee theme colors visible on mobile. Brown header background maintained. 'Ship to' bar uses theme colors. Mobile layout is consistent with desktop theme. No color conflicts.

testResults.title: Verify logo renders correctly on tablet viewport (768px - iPad)
testResults.reason: Not tested in this run. Would need to resize to 768px.

testResults.title: Verify whitelabeling renders correctly in Chrome browser (desktop)
testResults.reason: This run uses Firefox only. Chrome testing is handled by other suite runs.

testResults.title: Verify whitelabeling renders correctly in Firefox browser (desktop)
testResults.notes: Verified: Full white labeling renders correctly in Firefox desktop. Logo, favicon, theme colors, footer links all display correctly. Main navigation shows catalog categories. No Firefox-specific rendering issues for white labeling elements. 0 console errors on homepage. Only known issue: /catalog direct navigation causes blank page (MIME type block - not WL-specific).

testResults.title: Verify whitelabeling renders correctly in Edge browser (desktop)
testResults.reason: This run uses Firefox only.

testResults.title: Verify whitelabeling renders correctly on iPhone Safari (iOS)
testResults.reason: Requires real device or BrowserStack. Not available in this session.

testResults.title: Verify whitelabeling renders correctly on Android Chrome (mobile)
testResults.reason: Requires real device or BrowserStack.

testResults.title: Verify logo has proper alt text for accessibility
testResults.notes: Logo has alt='B2B-store' (generic store name). When org-specific logo is active (e.g., Coffee shop), alt text should reflect the organization. Additionally, 17 images on page lack alt text entirely. See BUG-35-001 and BUG-35-003.

testResults.title: Verify main menu is keyboard navigable
testResults.reason: Blocked by FWL-021. Main menu shows catalog categories, not custom link list. Catalog category menubar has role='menubar' with role='menuitem' children (40 items), which is correct ARIA structure.

testResults.title: Verify footer links are keyboard navigable
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer links are standard anchor elements with tabIndex accessible. Skip-to-footer link present ('Skip to footer' with href='#footer'). Footer sections use button elements for mobile accordion (keyboard accessible).

testResults.title: Verify focus indicators visible on all branded interactive elements
testResults.reason: Visual focus indicator testing requires manual keyboard interaction and visual inspection. Partially verified: Skip links present, interactive elements have cursor:pointer.

testResults.title: Verify screen reader announces main menu structure (parent and child links)
testResults.notes: Footer <nav> lacks aria-label. Navigation menubar has proper ARIA roles (menubar, menuitem). Main navigation has aria-label='Main navigation'. However, footer nav has no aria-label, making it indistinguishable for screen readers. See BUG-35-002.

testResults.title: Verify high contrast mode compatibility with whitelabeling theme
testResults.reason: Requires Windows High Contrast Mode activation. Not testable via Playwright.

testResults.title: Verify pageContext query performance - single call vs multiple queries
testResults.reason: Requires GraphQL playground to execute and measure individual queries.

testResults.title: Verify storefront page load time with whitelabeling enabled - under 3 seconds
testResults.notes: Verified: Homepage loaded within expected time (under 3 seconds observed). All GraphQL requests returned 200. No failed network requests. Logo, favicon, and theme CSS loaded successfully. Page was interactive within 2 seconds.

testResults.title: Verify logo image loading with lazy loading or optimization
testResults.notes: Verified: Logo image from /api/files/ endpoint. Natural size 900x593px rendered at 68x45px (CSS scaled down). Image loads via API file endpoint. No blocking behavior observed. Logo appears quickly.

testResults.title: Verify favicon caching to avoid repeated requests
testResults.notes: Verified: Favicons are inline data URIs (base64 encoded in the HTML). No additional HTTP requests needed for favicons. This is an efficient caching strategy - zero network overhead for favicon display.

testResults.title: Verify theme CSS caching and efficient loading
testResults.notes: Verified: 175 CSS custom properties loaded for Coffee theme. Theme colors applied via CSS variables on :root. CSS loaded from hashed asset files (index-CrC56xBw.js, vendor-GH7P-2sI.js) which enables long-term caching. No duplicate CSS loads observed.

testResults.title: Verify no FOUC when loading themed page
testResults.notes: Verified: No FOUC observed during page load. Coffee theme colors applied immediately. No flash of default theme. CSS variables loaded in-line. Theme transition during org switch was also smooth with no flash of wrong branding.

testResults.title: Verify multiple concurrent organization users can access branding without conflicts
testResults.reason: Requires two separate browser sessions. Only one Firefox session available in this run.

testResults.title: Verify branding persists after session timeout and re-login
testResults.reason: Requires waiting for session timeout or manual session invalidation. Time-intensive test.

testResults.title: Verify branding data is not exposed in client-side code or APIs for other organizations
testResults.notes: Verified: Network requests show only GraphQL calls to /graphql endpoint. All returned 200. No cross-org data leakage visible in network tab. Logo URLs are specific to the active org context. Switching orgs fetches new branding data via fresh GraphQL calls.

testResults.title: Verify no broken images when logo or favicon URL is invalid
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to set invalid logo URL.

testResults.title: Verify graceful handling when main menu link list does not exist
testResults.notes: Verified implicitly: Tested orgs appear to have no MainMenuLinkListName configured (or empty). Application handles this gracefully by showing catalog categories. No crash, no console error, no layout break.

testResults.title: Verify graceful handling when footer link list does not exist
testResults.notes: Verified: Footer displays correctly for both tested orgs. If footer link list name were invalid, fallback behavior would apply. No errors observed in footer rendering.

testResults.title: Verify logo upload validation - accepted file formats (PNG SVG JPEG)
testResults.reason: Admin SPA upload test.

testResults.title: Verify logo upload validation - rejected file formats (GIF BMP TXT)
testResults.reason: Admin SPA upload test.

testResults.title: Verify logo upload validation - file size limit (max 2MB)
testResults.reason: Admin SPA upload test.

testResults.title: Verify favicon upload validation - accepted file format (ICO PNG)
testResults.reason: Admin SPA upload test.

testResults.title: Verify organization-specific branding applies only to that organization's users
testResults.notes: Verified: Switching from 'Bence and Family' to 'Coffee shop' org changed the logo. Each org has its own branding context. The org switcher correctly updates the branding. Cart isolation also confirmed (cart count changed on org switch). Ship-to addresses changed. No cross-contamination between orgs.

testResults.title: Verify anonymous users (not signed in) see default branding
testResults.reason: Would require signing out and testing anonymous state. Deferred to avoid disrupting the signed-in session for other tests.

testResults.title: Verify branding updates in Admin reflect on storefront without cache issues
testResults.reason: Requires Admin SPA to update branding and verify storefront refresh.

testResults.title: Verify branding consistency across all storefront pages (homepage catalog product cart account)
testResults.notes: Partially verified: Homepage branding is consistent (logo, favicon, theme, footer). However, /catalog page renders BLANK in Firefox (JS module MIME type error). Cannot verify branding consistency on catalog, product, cart, or account pages in Firefox. This is a critical Firefox-specific issue affecting ALL storefront content pages beyond homepage. See known issue KI-001.

details: JavaScript Error: Loading module from 'https://vcst-qa-storefront.govirto.com/assets/index-DT1lESh8.js' was blocked because of a disallowed MIME type ('text/html') [on /catalog page only]

networkSummary.failedDetails: NS_BINDING_ABORTED on 2 GraphQL requests (Firefox navigation cancellation, not actual failures)

accessibilityFindings.footerNavAriaLabel: MISSING - no aria-label on footer nav
accessibilityFindings.keyboardNavigation: Skip links present, menubar has ARIA roles, interactive elements are keyboard accessible
